instruction_set.title: Retrieval Model Upgrade, Real Embeddings, Ablations, and Scientific Evaluation

metadata.phase_context: Neural Search v0.3 established the foundation for real-corpus scientific search: benchmark suite versioning, provenance-aware normalized schemas, scientific label extraction rules, adversarial benchmark design, and analysis affordance taxonomy.
metadata.v0_4_north_star: Upgrade Neural Search from a provenance-aware retrieval system into a scientifically evaluable hybrid retrieval engine with real embedding providers, field-specific embeddings, interpretable retrieval ablations, and robust evaluation over demo, real-corpus, and adversarial benchmarks.
metadata.strategic_warning: Do not jump into large foundation-model training yet. v0.4 is about making retrieval strong, measurable, explainable, and extensible. Learned latent models belong later, after the retrieval and evaluation substrate is stable.

global_rules.rule: Do not tune only for the v0.2 demo benchmark.
Do not remove or weaken provenance/evidence requirements from v0.3.
Do not make embeddings opaque; every result must still expose interpretable evidence.
Do not build frontend features unless absolutely necessary for CLI/report visibility.
Do not train a large neural foundation model in v0.4.
Add embedding providers behind interfaces so local, scientific, and hosted embeddings can be swapped.
Support field-specific embeddings rather than one undifferentiated text blob.
Add retrieval ablation reports so improvements are measurable and falsifiable.
Preserve benchmark suite separation: demo_v02, real_corpus, adversarial.
Prefer small fixture-backed tests over live-network-dependent tests.

goals.goal: Introduce real embedding providers beyond hashing placeholders.
Add field-specific embedding support for datasets and papers.
Add hybrid retrieval fusion over lexical, ontology, semantic, provenance, usability, analysis-fit, and negative-constraint scores.
Add ablation reports comparing retrieval heads and weighting schemes.
Improve real-corpus and adversarial benchmark signal quality.
Add relevance-label fixtures or lightweight human-review workflow for scientific query evaluation.
Prepare clean extension points for v0.5 deterministic NWB/BIDS scientific fingerprints.

non_goals.item: No paper-dataset graph implementation yet unless minimal linking primitives are already stable.

definition_of_done.item: Embedding provider interface exists and includes at least HashingEmbeddingProvider and one real local provider.
Field-specific embeddings exist for datasets and papers.
Embeddings can be cached, reloaded, and reused deterministically.
Hybrid retrieval can run with configurable retrieval-head weights.
Ablation reports compare lexical-only, ontology-only, semantic-only, and hybrid retrieval.
Benchmark runner can emit score decomposition and ablation metrics for demo_v02, real_corpus, and adversarial suites.
At least 25 real-corpus benchmark queries have structured expected labels or expected records.
At least 30 adversarial queries remain preserved and runnable.
Tests cover embedding providers, field embedding generation, cache roundtrips, scoring fusion, ablation reports, and benchmark compatibility.

architecture_principles.principle: Embeddings should improve recall and semantic matching, but never replace ontology, provenance, or evidence.
Different scientific fields within a record should be embedded separately because title similarity, task similarity, analysis-goal similarity, and paper-abstract similarity mean different things.
Retrieval quality should be measured with ablations, not vibes.
A search result should answer: why matched, why ranked here, what evidence supports it, what fields are missing, and what constraints were violated or satisfied.

workstream.title: Embedding Provider Interface
workstream.objective: Build a pluggable embedding provider system that supports the current hashing provider and at least one real local embedding provider.

tasks.task: Inspect current latent/embedding code and avoid breaking existing APIs.
Add a base embedding provider interface.
Keep HashingEmbeddingProvider as a deterministic fallback.
Add SentenceTransformerEmbeddingProvider behind optional dependency extras.
Gracefully fail with clear message if sentence-transformers is not installed.
Add tests for provider dimensions, determinism where applicable, batch embedding, and normalization.
Document how to install optional embedding extras.

acceptance_criteria.item: Hashing provider still works without optional dependencies.
SentenceTransformer provider works when optional dependencies are installed.
Embedding calls are tested without requiring network downloads in CI, using mocks or tiny fixtures if needed.
Provider metadata is saved with generated embeddings.

workstream.title: Field-Specific Embeddings
workstream.objective: Generate and store embeddings for specific scientific fields rather than one merged text blob.

tasks.task: Add FieldEmbeddingRecord model or equivalent.
Add embedding generation CLI for normalized corpus records.
Add embedding cache format with provider metadata, model name, dimension, created_at, source record ID, and field name.
Add cache loading and validation.
Add tests for cache roundtrip and field selection.

suggested_cli.command: python -m neural_search.embeddings.build_index --input data/corpus/normalized --out data/indexes/embeddings --provider sentence-transformer --model sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2

acceptance_criteria.item: Dataset and paper records can produce field-specific embeddings.
Embedding cache can be rebuilt or reused.
Missing optional fields do not crash embedding generation.
Embedding metadata prevents mixing incompatible providers/dimensions silently.

workstream.title: Hybrid Retrieval Fusion
workstream.objective: Upgrade retrieval to combine lexical, ontology, semantic, provenance, usability, analysis-fit, and negative-constraint heads using configurable weights.

tasks.task: Inspect current scoring decomposition from v0.3.
Add configurable retrieval weights in YAML or Python config.
Add semantic score from field-specific embeddings.
Support query-field routing: task-heavy queries should compare task/analysis fields; paper-like queries should compare abstracts.
Keep score breakdown visible in result output.
Add tests for weight changes altering rankings in expected ways.
Add tests ensuring negative constraints cannot be buried by high semantic similarity.

acceptance_criteria.item: Hybrid retrieval works with and without real embeddings.
Score breakdown remains visible.
Configurable weights are tested.
Negative constraints still strongly penalize or filter invalid results.

workstream.title: Ablation and Evaluation Reports
workstream.objective: Add evaluation reports that compare retrieval configurations and show where improvements come from.

tasks.task: Add ablation runner that can run one or more benchmark suites.
Emit JSON and Markdown reports.
Include per-query failure analysis.
Include top changed rankings between ablation modes.
Add tests using a tiny benchmark fixture.

acceptance_criteria.item: Ablation report shows which retrieval heads matter.
Reports are deterministic on fixture data.
Failures are visible and categorized.

workstream.title: Scientific Query Intent and Field Routing Design
workstream.objective: Design how natural language scientific queries should be parsed into retrieval intents, constraints, and field-specific search routes.

tasks.task: Create docs/QUERY_INTENT_AND_FIELD_ROUTING.md.
Define query intent categories and examples.
Define which score heads should be weighted for each intent.
Define which fields should be embedded/searched for each intent.
Define how negative constraints should be parsed and represented.
Define fallback behavior for ambiguous queries.
Give Codex implementation-ready pseudo-structures.

acceptance_criteria.item: Codex can implement query routing directly from the document.
Document covers dataset, paper, analysis, and exclusion-heavy queries.
Document preserves interpretability and provenance requirements.

workstream.title: Real-Corpus Benchmark Expansion
workstream.objective: Expand the real-corpus benchmark into a meaningful evaluation set, without relying on demo-only data.

tasks.task: Review current real_corpus benchmark file from v0.3.
Add or revise at least 25 real-corpus benchmark queries.
Each query should include expected labels and, where known, expected records.
Include queries across DANDI, OpenNeuro, and OpenAlex-style records.
Include paper-to-dataset and dataset-to-paper style queries.
Include analysis-affordance queries.
Document failure modes that are acceptable during v0.4.

query_mix.item: 5 paper-dataset linking or exploratory synthesis queries

acceptance_criteria.item: Real-corpus benchmark is distinct from demo_v02 and adversarial suites.
Queries are scientifically plausible and not synthetic metric fluff.
Expected labels are structured enough for Codex evaluation metrics.

workstream.title: Embedding Provider Recommendation and Retrieval Science Notes
workstream.objective: Define which embedding models are appropriate for each retrieval use case and what their limitations are.

tasks.task: Create docs/EMBEDDING_MODEL_STRATEGY.md.
Compare generic sentence-transformer embeddings, scientific paper embeddings, biomedical embeddings, and future custom neural-data embeddings.
Recommend default v0.4 provider choices.
Define when not to trust semantic embeddings.
Define evaluation criteria for future providers.
Explain why field-specific embeddings are required.

recommended_defaults.default: Use HashingEmbeddingProvider for deterministic tests and CI.
Use sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 or similar local provider for first semantic retrieval pass.
Prepare for SPECTER-style provider for paper abstracts, but make it optional.

acceptance_criteria.item: Document makes realistic recommendations without hype.
Document explains limitations of generic embeddings for neuroscience search.
Document gives Codex clear provider priorities.

workstream.title: Human Review and Relevance Labeling Protocol
workstream.objective: Design a lightweight protocol for manually reviewing search results so retrieval can be evaluated against human scientific judgment.

tasks.task: Create docs/HUMAN_RELEVANCE_LABELING_PROTOCOL.md.
Define relevance labels: exact, relevant, partially_relevant, wrong_modality, wrong_task, wrong_species, missing_required_data, unclear.
Define review format for top-k results.
Define how reviewed judgments should be stored.
Define how reviewed labels should feed evaluation metrics.
Add examples for 3 to 5 queries.

acceptance_criteria.item: Protocol can be used by the project owner without a custom UI.
Protocol supports JSONL or YAML review files.
Labels distinguish wrong results from partially useful scientific results.

steps.step: Confirm field-specific embedding priorities for Codex.
Wire semantic score into retrieval scoring.
Codex adds metrics for exclusion correctness and analysis-affordance matching if missing.
Run all benchmark suites and ablation modes.
Codex optionally adds parser for reviewed labels if earlier phases are stable.

optional_commands.command: python -m neural_search.embeddings.build_index --input data/corpus/normalized --out data/indexes/embeddings --provider sentence-transformer --model sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2

fallbacks.fallback: If sentence-transformers is unavailable, CI should still pass using hashing provider.
If live corpora are unavailable, use normalized fixture corpus.
If adversarial benchmark is intentionally difficult, failures should be reported, not hidden.

prompt.title: Claude Master Prompt for Neural Search v0.4

handoff_prompts.prompt:
You are working on Neural Search v0.4.
Context: v0.3 established benchmark versioning, provenance-aware normalized schema, scientific extraction rules, adversarial benchmark design, and analysis affordance taxonomy. Codex will handle most backend implementation.
Your mission: Design the scientific retrieval strategy for v0.4: query intent parsing, field-specific retrieval routing, embedding model strategy, real-corpus benchmark expansion, and human relevance labeling.
Do not: - build frontend features - tune only the demo benchmark - remove provenance/evidence requirements - train a large model - add synthetic-only metric inflation
Deliverables: 1. docs/QUERY_INTENT_AND_FIELD_ROUTING.md 2. docs/EMBEDDING_MODEL_STRATEGY.md 3. data/eval/benchmark_queries_real_corpus.yaml expanded to at least 25 meaningful queries 4. docs/REAL_CORPUS_BENCHMARK_GUIDE.md 5. docs/HUMAN_RELEVANCE_LABELING_PROTOCOL.md
Key requirements: - Define query intent categories. - Define which fields should be searched for each query type. - Define recommended score-head weights for each query type. - Define how negative constraints should be represented. - Define field-specific embedding strategy. - Define real-corpus benchmark queries that test scientific usefulness. - Define a lightweight human review protocol for top-k relevance judgments.
Acceptance criteria: - Codex can implement routing and scoring changes directly from your docs. - Real-corpus benchmark is meaningfully different from demo_v02 and adversarial. - Human review protocol is usable without a frontend. - All docs preserve provenance, confidence, and interpretability as first-class requirements.

prompt.title: Codex Backend Prompt for Neural Search v0.4

handoff_prompts.prompt:
You are working on Neural Search v0.4.
Context: v0.3 established benchmark suites, normalized provenance-aware schema, extraction rules, adversarial benchmark design, and analysis affordances. Your task is to upgrade retrieval infrastructure.
Your mission: Implement real embedding infrastructure, field-specific embeddings, hybrid retrieval fusion, and ablation reporting while preserving v0.3 provenance and score decomposition.
Do not: - build frontend features - tune only the demo benchmark - remove score explanations - make sentence-transformers a hard dependency for base install - train a large model - overwrite previous benchmark outputs
Required implementation areas:
1. Embedding provider interface: - HashingEmbeddingProvider - optional SentenceTransformerEmbeddingProvider - provider metadata - batch embedding - tests and clear optional dependency handling
2. Field-specific embeddings: - dataset title, description, tasks, behavioral_events, modalities, brain_regions, analysis_goals, data_standards, combined_scientific_summary - paper title, abstract, extracted_labels, combined_scientific_summary - cache format with provider, model, dimension, field, source record ID, created_at - cache validation
3. Hybrid retrieval fusion: - configurable score weights - semantic score from field-specific embeddings - preserve lexical, ontology, provenance, usability, analysis_fit, negative_constraint, final_score - prevent negative constraints from being buried by semantic similarity
4. Ablation reports: - lexical_only - ontology_only - semantic_only - ontology_plus_semantic - hybrid_default - hybrid_no_provenance - hybrid_no_analysis_fit - hybrid_no_negative_constraints - JSON and Markdown outputs
5. Tests: - provider interface - hashing provider - mocked sentence-transformer provider - field embedding cache roundtrip - semantic score integration - weight config changes ranking - negative constraints remain strong - ablation runner on fixture benchmark
Suggested commands: python -m pip install -e ".[dev]" pytest -q ruff check . python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite demo_v02 python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite real_corpus python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite adversarial python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_ablation --suite demo_v02 --modes lexical_only,ontology_only,semantic_only,hybrid_default
Acceptance criteria: - Base install works without sentence-transformers. - Optional embedding install works when available. - Field-specific embeddings are generated and cached. - Hybrid retrieval uses semantic score without losing interpretability. - Ablation reports show which retrieval heads drive performance. - Existing v0.2/v0.3 tests continue to pass.

recommended_git_branches.branch: Claude query intent and field routing docs.
Codex field-specific embedding generation and cache.
Codex hybrid retrieval scoring and weights.

suggested_first_commits.commit: Add query intent and field routing docs.
Add embedding provider interface with hashing provider tests.
Add optional sentence-transformer provider with mocked tests.
Add field-specific embedding records and cache roundtrip.
Wire semantic score into hybrid retrieval.
Add configurable retrieval weights and tests.

items.item: Dataset similarity based on experimental structure.
Analysis affordance validation from file metadata.
Preparation for learned contrastive dataset embeddings.
